codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully ports Mission 3 runtime foundations into go-choir. Three new packages (internal/types, internal/events, internal/store) provide core runtime types, event vocabulary, and durable SQLite-backed storage. The code is idiomatic Go with comprehensive test coverage, proper error handling, and no regressions to existing Mission 2 browser contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the pattern of using modernc.org/sqlite for SQLite (same as existing auth store), with WAL mode and proper connection limits. This pattern should be documented in AGENTS.md for future storage implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/store/store.go uses 'modernc.org/sqlite' driver with PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL and SetMaxOpenConns(1), matching the auth store pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library documentation (.factory/library/runtime-foundation.md) was created and captures key design decisions including the rationale for why TaskBlocked is non-terminal (may be retried). This is good practice that should be encouraged for foundational features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: runtime-foundation.md clearly documents: 'Blocked is non-terminal — blocked tasks may be retried or resolved when the provider recovers.'

summary: Feature runtime-types-store-foundation passes review. The implementation adds 2265 lines across 7 new files (3 packages with tests and documentation). All 36 new tests pass, go vet is clean, and builds succeed. The code correctly implements: (1) TaskState lifecycle with Terminal()/Valid() predicates, (2) EventBus with actor/cause filtering and supervisor attention logic, (3) SQLite persistence with WAL mode, restart-safe recovery, and proper error handling. No security concerns identified in the persistence layer. The worker followed skill procedure correctly with no deviations.
